title: Coverage Attestation
description: Structural schema companion for contract: coverage-attestation/v0 (PROPOSED — see ADR-0004). An independent, supersedable claim about what an artifact or scoped subject actually covers. Completeness is asserted per scope claim; absence of an attestation is never itself a claim. Behavioral rules (fail-open-but-honest presence, fail-loud malformation, protection of scope boundary values) are enforced by consumers.

capabilities.description: Host-less capability tokens. Must include the exact opaque coverage-attestation contract token.

attestation_id.description: Attestation identity. UUID URNs are recommended for generated attestations.

supersedes.description: attestation_id of a prior attestation over the same subject that this one replaces (late-arrival re-attestation).

subject.description: What is being attested. Prefer artifact_id linkage to a data-artifact/v0 descriptor; subject_uri supports non-descriptor subjects during adoption.

emitter.description: Who is making the claim. relation distinguishes a producer self-claim from an independent verifier/reconciler; one designated emitter per subject reconciles rather than re-derives.

as_of.description: When the assessment was made (ISO-8601). Later as_of over the same subject supersedes, in combination with supersedes.

coverage_state.description: Overall assessment across the claims. unknown is an honest verdict, not a default — a subject with no attestation has NO coverage_state.

claims.description: Per-scope coverage claims. Each claim carries its own basis and method; a single attestation may mix confirmed and inferred scopes.

basis.description: confirmed = positively verified against an authoritative enumeration; inferred = derived from indirect evidence. Consumers making destructive decisions MUST distinguish these.

volume.description: Quantitative coverage. Presence alone is insufficient — a scope can be present yet volume-deficient.

gaps.description: Explicitly known-uncovered scopes. Honest gaps here are what keeps fail-open presence trustworthy.

protection.description: Attestations inherit the most-restrictive export class of their subject and start from block_export. Scope boundary values (partition values, prefixes) are export-classed content; publish raw boundary values only when the effective export class permits it, otherwise use opaque scope tokens.

scope.description: A bounded slice of the subject: partition key/values, a time window, and/or a key prefix. At least one bound is required. Partition and prefix values are content; use opaque tokens unless the effective export class permits raw values.
